_meta.description: Practice problem translations keyed by initial_misions.id

perm_cat.title: Permutations of "CAT"
perm_cat.statement: Calculate the number of distinct permutations of the letters in the word “CAT”.

perm_book.title: Permutations of "BOOK"
perm_book.statement: Calculate the number of distinct permutations of the letters in the word “BOOK”.

comb_committee_3of10.title: Choosing a Committee of 3
comb_committee_3of10.statement: Find the number of ways to choose 3 students from a class of 10 to form a committee.

binom_coeff_x3_xy7.title: Coefficient of x³ in (x + y)⁷
binom_coeff_x3_xy7.statement: Find the coefficient of \(x^3\) in the expansion of \((x + y)^7\).

stars_bars_nonneg_5.title: Non-negative Solutions to x₁+x₂+x₃=5
stars_bars_nonneg_5.statement: Determine the number of non-negative integer solutions to the equation \(x_1 + x_2 + x_3 = 5\).

pigeonhole_birth_month.title: Birth Month Pigeonhole
pigeonhole_birth_month.statement: In a group of 50 people, how many people must be selected to guarantee that at least 2 share the same birth month (assuming 12 months)?

arrange_4_books.title: Arranging 4 Distinct Books
arrange_4_books.statement: Calculate the number of ways to arrange 4 distinct books on a shelf.

binom_coeff_x2y4_xy6.title: Coefficient of x²y⁴ in (x + y)⁶
binom_coeff_x2y4_xy6.statement: Find the coefficient of \(x^2y^4\) in the expansion of \((x + y)^6\).

balls_boxes_no_empty.title: Balls into Boxes (No Box Empty)
balls_boxes_no_empty.statement: In how many ways can 5 different balls be distributed into 3 different boxes such that no box is empty?

coin_flips_6_heads.title: Coin Flips with Exactly 6 Heads
coin_flips_6_heads.statement: A coin is flipped 10 times. How many sequences contain exactly 6 heads?

positive_int_solutions_4var.title: Positive Integer Solutions (4 Variables)
positive_int_solutions_4var.statement: How many positive integer solutions are there to \(x_1 + x_2 + x_3 + x_4 = 10\)?

perm_mississippi.title: Permutations of "MISSISSIPPI"
perm_mississippi.statement: Find the number of permutations of the word “MISSISSIPPI”.

binom_coeff_x4_1x10.title: Coefficient of x⁴ in (1 + x)¹⁰
binom_coeff_x4_1x10.statement: What is the coefficient of \(x^4\) in the expansion of \((1 + x)^{10}\)?

pigeonhole_mod7.title: Pigeonhole: Same Remainder mod 7
pigeonhole_mod7.statement: In a set of 100 integers, what is the minimum number of integers one must select to guarantee that two of them have the same remainder when divided by 7?

candies_min_one_each.title: Distributing Candies with a Minimum Each
candies_min_one_each.statement: Calculate the number of ways to distribute 6 identical candies to 3 distinct children such that each child gets at least 1 candy.

functions_4_objects_2_boxes.title: Functions from 4 Objects to 2 Boxes
functions_4_objects_2_boxes.statement: If n distinct objects are placed into k distinct boxes, how many ways can this be done if \(n = 4\) and \(k = 2\)?

sum_coeff_2x_3y_5.title: Sum of Coefficients in (2x − 3y)⁵
sum_coeff_2x_3y_5.statement: Find the sum of the coefficients in the expansion of \((2x - 3y)^5\).

committee_2men_2women.title: Committee with Exactly 2 Men and 2 Women
committee_2men_2women.statement: A committee of 4 people is to be formed from 5 men and 5 women. How many ways can this be done if the committee must contain exactly 2 men and 2 women?

prove_symmetry_identity.title: Proving C(n,k) = C(n, n−k)
prove_symmetry_identity.statement: Prove that \(\binom{n}{k} = \binom{n}{n-k}\) for \(n = 6\), \(k = 2\) and provide the numerical result.

sage_multiset_no_adj.title: Multiset Permutations, No Equal Letters Adjacent
sage_multiset_no_adj.statement:
A word contains \(a\) copies of A, \(b\) copies of B, and \(c\) copies of C. Count the number of distinct permutations of the word in which no two equal letters are adjacent.

Input: one line with three integers \(a\ b\ c\).
Output: a single integer — the count.

Example
Input: 2 1 1
Output: 6

sage_circular_arrangements.title: Circular Arrangements (up to Rotation)
sage_circular_arrangements.statement:
Given \(n\) distinct objects, count the number of distinct circular arrangements, where arrangements that differ only by rotation are considered the same.

Input: one line with the integer \(n\).
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input: 7
Output: 720

sage_circular_not_adjacent.title: Circular Arrangements with a Forbidden Adjacency
sage_circular_not_adjacent.statement:
Given \(n\) distinct objects arranged on a circle (up to rotation), count the circular arrangements in which two particular distinguished objects are not adjacent.

Input: one line with the integer \(n\) (\(n \ge 2\)).
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input: 6
Output: 48

sage_multinomial_coeff.title: Multinomial Coefficient from an Expansion
sage_multinomial_coeff.statement:
Find the coefficient of \(x^a y^b z^c\) in the expansion of \((x+y+z)^n\), where \(a+b+c=n\).

Input: one line with four integers \(n\ a\ b\ c\).
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input: 8 3 2 3
Output: 560

sage_bounded_stars_bars.title: Bounded Stars and Bars
sage_bounded_stars_bars.statement:
Count the number of nonnegative integer solutions to \(x_1 + x_2 + \cdots + x_k = n\) subject to \(x_i \le u_i\) for every \(i\).

Input: the first line has the integer \(n\); the second line has the \(k\) upper bounds \(u_1\ u_2\ \ldots\ u_k\), space-separated.
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input:
7
2 3 4
Output: 5

sage_surjections.title: Counting Surjections
sage_surjections.statement:
How many surjective (onto) functions are there from an \(n\)-element set to a \(k\)-element set?

Input: one line with two integers \(n\ k\).
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input: 8 4
Output: 40824

sage_set_partitions_k_blocks.title: Set Partitions into Exactly k Blocks
sage_set_partitions_k_blocks.statement:
Count the number of ways to partition an \(n\)-element set into exactly \(k\) nonempty, unlabeled subsets (a Stirling number of the second kind).

Input: one line with two integers \(n\ k\).
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input: 9 3
Output: 3025

sage_bell_number.title: Bell Numbers
sage_bell_number.statement:
Compute the Bell number \(B_n\): the total number of ways to partition an \(n\)-element set into any number of nonempty, unlabeled subsets.

Input: one line with the integer \(n\).
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input: 8
Output: 4140

sage_gaussian_binomial.title: Gaussian Binomial Coefficients
sage_gaussian_binomial.statement:
Compute the number of \(k\)-dimensional subspaces of \(\mathbb{F}_q^n\) (the Gaussian binomial coefficient \(\binom{n}{k}_q\)).

Input: one line with three integers \(q\ n\ k\).
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input: 2 6 3
Output: 1395

sage_derangements_r_fixed.title: Permutations with Exactly r Fixed Points
sage_derangements_r_fixed.statement:
Count the permutations of \(\{1, \ldots, n\}\) that have exactly \(r\) fixed points.

Input: one line with two integers \(n\ r\).
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input: 7 2
Output: 105

sage_poisson_binomial.title: Poisson-Binomial: Exact Probability of k Successes
sage_poisson_binomial.statement:
Given independent Bernoulli trials with success probabilities \(p_1, \ldots, p_n\), compute the exact probability of exactly \(k\) successes, as a reduced fraction.

Input: the first line has the probabilities \(p_1\ p_2\ \ldots\ p_n\) as fractions, space-separated; the second line has the integer \(k\).
Output: the exact probability as a reduced fraction (e.g. 11/24).

Example
Input:
1/2 1/3 1/4
2
Output: 11/24

sage_forced_nonempty_boxes.title: Onto Functions with Forced Nonempty Boxes
sage_forced_nonempty_boxes.statement:
Distribute \(n\) distinct balls into \(k\) distinct boxes so that every box ends up nonempty, where the first \(t\) boxes are additionally required to be nonempty.

Input: one line with three integers \(n\ k\ t\).
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input: 6 4 2
Output: 1560

sage_mixed_gen_func_coeff.title: Coefficient from a Mixed Generating Function
sage_mixed_gen_func_coeff.statement:
Find the coefficient of \(x^a y^b\) in \((1 + x + x^2)^m (1+y)^n\).

Input: one line with four integers \(m\ n\ a\ b\).
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input: 4 5 3 2
Output: 120

sage_ordered_bell.title: Ordered Bell (Fubini) Numbers
sage_ordered_bell.statement:
Count the number of ordered set partitions of an \(n\)-element set (i.e. sequences of nonempty, disjoint blocks whose union is the whole set) — the ordered Bell / Fubini number.

Input: one line with the integer \(n\).
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input: 5
Output: 541

sage_exactly_r_distinct.title: Exactly r Distinct Values Among n Draws
sage_exactly_r_distinct.statement:
An urn contains \(m\) labeled colors. Draw \(n\) balls independently with replacement. Count the number of sequences in which exactly \(r\) distinct colors appear.

Input: one line with three integers \(m\ n\ r\).
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input: 4 5 3
Output: 1440

sage_restricted_compositions.title: Restricted Compositions via Generating Functions
sage_restricted_compositions.statement:
Count the number of integer solutions to \(x_1 + \cdots + x_k = n\) where each \(x_i\) must belong to a prescribed finite set \(S_i\).

Input: the first line has the integer \(k\) (number of variables); each of the next \(k\) lines lists the allowed values of \(S_i\), space-separated; the final line has the integer \(n\).
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input:
3
0 1
1 2
0 2
3
Output: 3

sage_perms_c_cycles.title: Permutations with a Given Number of Cycles
sage_perms_c_cycles.statement:
Count the permutations of \(n\) elements that have exactly \(c\) cycles (an unsigned Stirling number of the first kind).

Input: one line with two integers \(n\ c\).
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input: 6 2
Output: 274

sage_binomial_tail.title: Binomial Tail Probability (Symbolic)
sage_binomial_tail.statement:
Let \(X \sim \text{Binomial}(n, p)\). Compute \(\Pr[X \ge k]\) exactly as a reduced fraction (\(p\) is given as a fraction).

Input: one line with \(n\ p\ k\), where \(p\) is written as a fraction like 1/2.
Output: the exact probability as a reduced fraction.

Example
Input: 5 1/2 3
Output: 5/16

sage_binary_necklaces.title: Binary Necklaces (Burnside's Lemma)
sage_binary_necklaces.statement:
Count binary necklaces of length \(n\) up to rotation.
Input: 8
Output: 36

sage_necklaces_k_black.title: Binary Necklaces with a Fixed Number of Black Beads
sage_necklaces_k_black.statement:
Count the binary necklaces of length \(n\) with exactly \(k\) black beads, up to rotation.

Input: one line with two integers \(n\ k\).
Output: a single integer.

Example
Input: 10 3
Output: 12
